title: Dependency vulnerability remediation (Sprint 4)
description: Downstream-native security maintenance: targeted, semver-safe dependency updates. 8 audit findings → 2 (all 3 HIGH cleared). No `npm audit fix --force`, no major upgrades, no unrelated churn.

verification.summary: Resolved: HIGH next 16.2.2→16.2.10 (+ clears a postcss moderate); HIGH brace-expansion, js-yaml; MOD resend 6.10→6.18, svix, uuid; LOW @babel/core. Residual: 2 MODERATE (next + postcss) only fixed by a MAJOR next upgrade — NOT forced (documented). tsc 0, eslint clean, full 469/469, test:ai 124/124, regression 2/2, next build ✓; PR #12 Preview + regression green. PR OPEN — not merged (Preview-only per owner).

knownDifferences: 2 residual moderate advisories deferred to a dedicated Next major-upgrade PR.

rollbackSteps: Rollback: git revert 68f2d3c (or restore the prior package-lock.json). No runtime/behavior change beyond the patched libraries.
exclusions: Major Next.js upgrade (would resolve the 2 residual moderates) — deferred; requires codemods + full regression.
compatibilityNotes: next 16.2.10 is a patch within 16.2.x (Vercel/Node compatible).
Lockfile churn limited to the selected packages.

history.note: npm audit surfaced 8 vulns (3 high) on SC main ef618c6.
Full verification + Preview green. PR #12 OPEN, NOT merged (owner keeps Preview-only).
